item.title: Forcing the fit: MQM in the age of automated evaluation
item.description: MQM was the most robust answer we had for human evaluation. Automated evaluation is a different problem, and the seams are starting to show.Read more

item.title: Multilingual video localization, with the assets you’ve already built
item.description: When AV runs outside your TMS, terminology drifts and consistency breaks down. Phrase Studio closes that gap by running subtitling, dubbing, and voice-over inside the same workflow your text content…Read more

item.title: Every market is a new market
item.description: Your brand and your existing playbook will earn you a foothold in a new market. What they won’t do is carry you to sustained growth. Kevin O’Donnell on why international…Read more

item.title: Phrase is the Language Intelligence Platform.
item.description: Most announcements work like this. A company describes what it intends to build, frames it as a vision, and asks you to believe the future they’re describing. This post is…Read more

item.title: Should you build or buy your localization infrastructure?
item.description: The instinct to build is strongest in engineering-led companies. But maintaining a localization platform over time is a different problem than building a proof of concept — and most organizations…Read more

item.title: How AI and hyper-localization are changing international growth
item.description: The real opportunity in AI isn’t faster output — it’s reaching audiences that were previously too niche or too expensive to serve. Kevin O’Donnell explains why hyper-localization is becoming a…Read more

item.title: A model that generated billions in Europe didn’t survive the US market
item.description: The companies capturing real value from AI aren’t the ones with the biggest budgets or the most sophisticated tooling. They’re the ones that understood early that the technology was never…Read more

item.title: Why most companies don’t have an international dashboard, and what it’s costing them
item.description: When you break global revenue down by individual market, patterns emerge that should be driving strategic decisions. Most companies don’t have the reporting infrastructure to see them.Read more

item.title: Who should own international growth?
item.description: When no single leader owns international performance, investment scatters, team priorities diverge, and the signals that would tell leadership where to act never surface in a coherent way.Read more
